Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search the Airtable Relocations table for all Vectral Systems records. Discovers Kevin Tran (recReloc00000003, Completed, Portland OR to Denver CO, move Apr 19-20) with a duplicate record (rec_reloc_tran, also Completed, Portland to Denver, RELOC-2026-005, created Jan 2026). Discovers Danielle Osei (recReloc00000004, Completed, Phoenix AZ to Denver CO, move Apr 26-27) with a contradicting record (rec_reloc_osei, Cancelled, Phoenix to Denver, RELOC-2026-010, notes say client pulled relocation due to budget reallocation, cancellation email sent to Heartland on 2/13). Also finds Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya (all In Progress). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value Vectral Systems.
OE 2 - Search the Airtable Relocations table for all Mosaic Robotics records. Discovers Andre Washington (recReloc00000009, Completed, Detroit MI to Atlanta GA, move Apr 26-27), Tanya Reeves (rec_reloc_reeves, Completed, San Diego to Seattle), Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta, Completed with damage claim on prototype), Lena Kowalski (recReloc00000000037, In Progress, Detroit to Salt Lake City), Andre Mitchell (recReloc00000000038, In Progress, Columbus OH to Boise ID), Deshawn Hartley (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver, In Progress, Tampa to Denver), and Keiko Tanaka (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle, In Progress, Columbus OH to Seattle). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value Mosaic Robotics.
OE 3 - Search the Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all Vectral Systems records. Discovers pilot-affected records including three that are still incorrectly rejected: Maria Santos $550 Standing Desk (recStip000000018, Rejected, review notes say "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot stale $400 default limit, Vectral policy allows $600, Pending manual correction"), James Okafor $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray (recStip000000019, Rejected, review notes say "Auto-rejected, double bug: miscategorized as office_supplies and used stale $50 limit, Pending manual correction"), Lisa Park $130 Mesh WiFi System (recStip000000020, Rejected, review notes say "Auto-rejected stale $100 default limit, Vectral policy allows $150, Pending manual correction"). Also discovers already-corrected records: Danielle Osei $475 Ergonomic Chair (recStip000000016, Approved, manually corrected by Mina), Kevin Tran $185 Internet Setup (recStip000000017, Approved, manually corrected by Mina). Discovers duplicate records for each: rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair ($475, Approved), rec_stipend_kevin_tran_internet ($185, Approved), rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk ($550, Pending Approval), rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi ($130, Pending Approval). Discovers rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard ($89 Ergonomic Keyboard, Approved, flagged Duplicate Hash with SHA-256 identical to Andre Washington submission rec_stipend_andre_keyboard). Plus clean post-pilot records for Kevin Tran (recStip000000004 chair $895, recStip000000005 coworking $250 properly rejected, recStip000000006 webcam $189, recStip000000014 monitor arm $129 Pending). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Account, value Vectral Systems.
OE 4 - Search the Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all Mosaic Robotics records. Discovers three false-approval records from the pilot: Andre Washington $1200 Standing Desk (recStip000000021, Approved in error, Mosaic policy caps ergonomic at $800, $400 over limit, config bug with string comparison), Andre Washington $350 Coworking Membership (recStip000000022, Approved in error, coworking explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy, config bug with excluded_categories stored as string not array), Kenji Nakamura $135 Coworking Day Passes (recStip000000023, Approved in error, same exclusion bug). All three note MoveOps absorbing costs per Elena directive. Discovers duplicate records: rec_stipend_andre_desk ($1200, Approved, duplicate of recStip000000021), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork ($135, Approved, duplicate of recStip000000023). Discovers rec_stipend_andre_keyboard ($89 Ergonomic Keyboard, Pending Approval, flagged Duplicate Hash identical to Kevin Tran submission). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Account, value Mosaic Robotics.
OE 5 - List Linear issues in the expense autocategorizer project to find all relevant engineering tickets. Discovers: issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211, Pilot Monitoring Vectral and Mosaic, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214, P1 Fix Vectral config, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215, P1 Fix Mosaic config, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216, Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses, state_in_progress, priority 0 urgent, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, Harden policy ingestion pipeline, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218, Dashboard type validation for policy config editor, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_anh_nguyen), issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219, Define launch readiness checklist for features with automated financial decisions, state_todo, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, Stipend duplicate detection receipt hash fingerprinting, state_todo, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212, Remaining edge case coverage, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov). Tool: linear_list_issues with project proj_expense_autocategorizer.
OE 6 - Read comments on issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) to understand the full pilot incident timeline. Discovers: comment_false_rejection_incident (Samira reporting Danielle Osei $475 false rejection), comment_mina_two_incidents (Mina detailing Danielle Osei $475 chair auto-denied at $400 limit instead of $600, and Kevin Tran $185 internet auto-denied at $100 limit instead of $150, both manually overridden), comment_emeka_false_approvals (Emeka reporting Andre Washington $1200 desk approved despite $800 cap and $350 coworking approved despite exclusion, total risk at least $1,550), comment_monitoring_rca_update (Lena linking both incidents to policy ingestion pipeline ENG-209), comment_marcus_audit_request (Marcus demanding halt of auto-approvals and full audit), comment_elena_directive (Elena pausing auto-decisions, ordering full audit by 3/19, MoveOps absorbs $1,550, Mina to contact Vectral HR, Emeka to contact Mosaic HR), comment_samira_pause_confirmed (Samira confirming auto-decisions disabled March 18 for both clients). Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_pilot_monitoring.
OE 7 - Read comments on issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216) to find Dmitri's audit-related findings. Discovers comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery (2026-03-15) detailing three questionable receipt submissions found during the post-pause audit: Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, Paid 2026-03-08) and Andre Washington $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Pending) share identical SHA-256 hash a3f7c8d1e9b2, and Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, Pending, BrightLoop Analytics) shares same PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. Total flagged: $223 ($89 already paid out, $134 pending). Dmitri notes the existing system lacks duplicate receipt hash or PDF metadata detection. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_audit_expenses.
OE 8 - Read comments on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) to check the status of the duplicate receipt investigation. Discovers comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context (2026-03-15) noting: Airtable records identified as rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand. QuickBooks holds placed by Marcus on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme $45). AM verification requests sent to Vectral (Mina to Rachel Whitfield), Mosaic (Emeka to Tamara Byrd), and BrightLoop (Emeka to Tessa Moreno). Dmitri planned to begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation on Monday with Lena on ingestion pipeline changes. Ticket remains in state_todo. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_stipend_duplicate_detection.
OE 9 - Read comments on issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) and issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) to verify that config fixes were deployed. On ENG-214, discovers comment_lena_vectral_fix_plan (Lena beginning immediate config corrections, updating all Vectral limits from defaults to confirmed values, eliminating try/catch fallback). On ENG-215, discovers comment_lena_mosaic_fix_plan (correcting excluded_categories to array, ensuring max_amount fields are numbers, adding type guards) and comment_lena_deploy_verified (2026-03-18, posted on ENG-215) confirming both Vectral and Mosaic config corrections deployed to production with verification: Danielle Osei $475 ergonomic chair now correctly approved ($475 less than $600), Kevin Tran $185 internet correctly approved but setup fee edge case still needs separate ticket, Andre Washington $1200 desk correctly denied ($1200 greater than $800), Andre Washington coworking correctly denied (exclusion list working). Auto-decisions disabled so all route to review queue. Lena states she is closing ENG-214 and now starting ENG-217 pipeline hardening. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_p1_vectral_config, then linear_list_comments with issueId issue_p1_mosaic_malformed.
OE 10 - Read comments on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) to check current pipeline hardening status. Discovers comment_dmitri_audit_results (2026-03-20, posted on this issue) with the full audit: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct (83%), 5 false rejections (all Vectral: Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh wifi), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic: Andre Washington $1200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes), total financial risk $885. Also notes James Okafor revealed a categorization bug (keyboard tray not in ergonomic keyword list, separate from config problem). Ticket remains state_in_progress. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_pipeline_hardening.
OE 11 - Read comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) to discover the schema validation tests relevant to pipeline readiness. Discovers comment_dmitri_validation_tests (2026-03-20) listing 6 schema validation tests implemented in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback. All six pass with corrected configurations but all fail with original production configurations, confirming they would have caught both root causes before deployment. Lena is incorporating these tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate. Remaining ENG-212 work includes edge case tests for categorization, vendor abbreviation handling, and internet setup vs monthly distinction. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_edge_cases_backlog.
OE 12 - Search emails for threads related to the expense categorizer pilot, false rejections, and false approvals. Discovers: Samira's audit summary email to Elena listing 47 expenses with 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure; Marcus's email raising financial urgency about the Mosaic $1,550 incorrect approvals and demanding immediate halt; Emeka's email to Samira and Marcus reporting Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura false approvals with specific dollar amounts; and Samira's detailed audit spreadsheet email to Marcus confirming $885 total risk with MoveOps absorbing per Elena's directive. Tool: search_emails with query related to expense categorizer or pilot or false rejection or auto-approve.
OE 13 - Search emails for threads related to duplicate receipt findings and financial holds. Discovers: Dmitri's email to Marcus flagging three questionable receipt submissions (Kevin Tran $89, Andre Washington $89, Jordan Ekwueme $45 from TechComfort Supply), Marcus's reply confirming QuickBooks holds placed on STIP-2026-0094 ($89 Andre Washington, not yet disbursed) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45 Jordan Ekwueme, not yet disbursed) while Kevin Tran's $89 was already paid out on 2026-03-08, Marcus's email to Mina requesting Vectral HR verification of Kevin Tran's receipt legitimacy, Marcus's email to Emeka requesting Mosaic HR (Tamara Byrd) and BrightLoop HR (Tessa Moreno) verification, and Emeka's outreach email to Tamara Byrd about Andre Washington's $89 receipt. Tool: search_emails with query related to duplicate receipt or TechComfort or stipend hold or STIP-2026.
OE 14 - Search Slack messages in the engineering channel (C003) for discussions about the expense categorizer pilot. Discovers the full development and incident history: Samira's original proposal for auto-categorization, Chloe's operational requirements posted by Samira (Vectral: equipment $500, furniture $400, ergonomic $600, internet $150/mo, coworking excluded, office supplies $75; Mosaic: equipment $1000, furniture $800, ergonomic $800, internet $200/mo, coworking excluded, office supplies $100), Lena's architecture and keyword specifications, Dmitri's edge case test plans, progress updates, and the pilot launch and pause discussions. These confirm the correct policy limits that the pipeline should enforce. Tool: conversations_search_messages with search_query related to expense categorizer or pilot or ExpenseBot, or conversations_history with channel_id C003.
OE 15 - Search Slack or email for operations and billing threads about Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics that would affect how automated processing reads the records. Discovers the Heartland Movers Q1 billing discrepancy discussion in the operations channel (C006) where Chloe posted a full review and in the finance channel (C005) where Marcus logged the payment hold: Heartland billed for 8 moves at $1,600 each ($12,800) but only 5 are legitimate ($8,000), with 3 disputed ($4,800). Disputed moves include Danielle Osei (Vectral, Phoenix to Denver listed as Cancelled, RELOC-2026-010, confirming the cancellation also recorded in rec_reloc_osei), Lily Marchetti (Canopy Health, Nashville to Chicago, also Cancelled), and Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack, handled by Swift not Heartland). This Heartland billing thread cross-references and supports the Cancelled status for Danielle Osei's relocation. Tool: conversations_search_messages with search_query Heartland or billing Vectral Mosaic, or search_emails with query Heartland Q1 invoice.
OE 16 - Search CRM deals for Vectral Systems to check for client risk and billing context. Discovers deal_vectral_q2 ($11,000, Q2 2026 Relocations for Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei, stage contractsent), deal_vectral_april_2026 ($15,900, 3 April relocations for Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya, closedwon), and deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (At Risk stage, Rachel Whitfield demanded 15% discount citing three service failures: ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $89 keyboard flagged in fraud audit, and onboarding timeline issues under Catalina Dubois in Nov 2024). Rachel states Vectral is evaluating two competitor providers and needs a response by end of week for a board meeting where vendor renewal decisions will be presented. Mina is holding response pending internal alignment on counter-offer strategy. This represents active client and reimbursement risk for the risks section. Tool: crm_search_deals with company_id company_vectral or dealname Vectral.
OE 17 - Search CRM deals for Mosaic Robotics to check for data discrepancies and context. Discovers deal_mosaic_q2 ($4,500, stage closedwon, description says "Andre Washington, Robotics Engineer, from Columbus, GA to Seattle, WA") which contradicts the Airtable relocation record recReloc00000009 (Detroit MI to Atlanta GA) and Tamara Byrd's original email requesting relocation from Detroit to Atlanta. The CRM deal has the wrong origin city (Columbus GA instead of Detroit MI) and wrong destination city (Seattle WA instead of Atlanta GA). This discrepancy would cause automated processing to read the wrong route for this move. Tool: crm_search_deals with company_id company_mosaic or dealname Mosaic.
OE 18 - Look up contact details for Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen to retrieve their email addresses for the summary email. Discovers lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com (contact_id moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, Software Engineer, San Francisco), dmitri.volkov@moveops.com (contact_id moveops_dmitri_volkov, Software Engineer, San Francisco remote), anh.nguyen@moveops.com (contact_id moveops_anh_nguyen, Software Engineer, Austin remote). Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query Lena Bjorkstrom, then Dmitri Volkov, then Anh Nguyen.
OE 19 - Check Samira Tariq's calendar for early next week to find available time for a 30-minute meeting with Lena, Dmitri, and Anh. Early next week resolves to Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28, 2026 from the fixed universe date of April 26, 2026 (Sunday). Tool: calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime 2026-04-27 and end_datetime 2026-04-28 (or through end of April 28) to check for open slots.
OE 20 - Update the three still-rejected Vectral Systems stipend records to Approved status with corrective review notes, since proof is clear (all three have notes stating Pending manual correction and the correct Vectral policy limits confirm amounts are within policy). Records: recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550 Standing Desk, change Approval Status from Rejected to Approved, add note that Vectral ergonomic limit is $600 and $550 is within policy), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, change Approval Status from Rejected to Approved, add note that the pipeline miscategorized as office_supplies but the Airtable record already has the correct category Ergonomic Keyboard Tray and $82 is within Vectral ergonomic limit of $600), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130 Mesh WiFi System, change from Rejected to Approved, add note that Vectral internet equipment limit is $150 and $130 is within policy). Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001, records array containing the three updates.
OE 21 - Update the Danielle Osei relocation records in Airtable to flag the status contradiction. Record recReloc00000004 shows Status Completed with move dates Apr 26-27, but record rec_reloc_osei shows Status Cancelled with notes referencing RELOC-2026-010 and a cancellation email sent to Heartland on 2/13 due to budget reallocation. The Heartland Q1 billing dispute also lists this move as cancelled and disputed. Add review notes to both records flagging the contradiction so the pipeline does not treat the Completed record as authoritative without resolution. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01.
OE 22 - Update the duplicate Kevin Tran relocation records to flag the duplication. Record recReloc00000003 (created 2026-03-31, move dates Apr 19-20, Completed) and rec_reloc_tran (created 2026-01-08, move date Jan 26, RELOC-2026-005, Completed) both represent Kevin Tran at Vectral Systems relocating from Portland to Denver with the same coordinator Javier Morales. Add review notes to both records identifying them as duplicate entries for the same relocation so the pipeline does not count or process this move twice. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01.
OE 23 - Update the duplicate stipend record pairs to flag them as duplicates that the pipeline should not process as separate transactions. Six pairs identified: (1) Kevin Tran Internet $185, recStip000000017 and rec_stipend_kevin_tran_internet, (2) Danielle Osei Chair $475, recStip000000016 and rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair, (3) Maria Santos Desk $550, recStip000000018 and rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, (4) Lisa Park WiFi $130, recStip000000020 and rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi, (5) Andre Washington Desk $1200, recStip000000021 and rec_stipend_andre_desk, (6) Kenji Nakamura Coworking $135, recStip000000023 and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork. Add review notes to each record flagging it as a duplicate pair entry. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001 (multiple calls needed, max 10 records per call).
OE 24 - Update the duplicate receipt and audit-hold stipend records to ensure they do not read as clean for automated processing. Records: rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $89, already flagged Duplicate Hash, already Paid on 2026-03-08), rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Andre Washington, Mosaic, $89, flagged Duplicate Hash, Pending Approval, QuickBooks hold STIP-2026-0094). Add or reinforce review notes indicating these are under active duplicate receipt investigation (ENG-220) and should not be auto-processed. The three Mosaic false-approval records (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023) already have detailed review notes documenting the errors and Elena's directive to absorb costs, so they are already marked as audit records. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001.
OE 25 - Create a comment on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) documenting the 6 duplicate stipend record pairs discovered during the release readiness pass, the status of the cross-company duplicate receipt investigation (Kevin Tran Vectral $89 and Andre Washington Mosaic $89 with identical SHA-256 hash, Kevin Tran already paid out), and the current Airtable annotation status. Note that this ticket should remain open until the hash fingerprinting system is implemented and all duplicate pairs are fully reconciled. Tool: linear_create_comment with issueId issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, body containing the findings summary.
OE 26 - Create a comment on issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) or issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216) documenting open items found during the release readiness pass that block relaunch: (1) Danielle Osei relocation status contradiction between recReloc00000004 (Completed) and rec_reloc_osei (Cancelled), supported by Heartland Q1 billing dispute, (2) Kevin Tran duplicate relocation records recReloc00000003 and rec_reloc_tran, (3) Andre Washington CRM deal discrepancy where deal_mosaic_q2 lists route as Columbus GA to Seattle WA but actual route per Airtable and email is Detroit MI to Atlanta GA, (4) three false rejections now corrected (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park), (5) six duplicate stipend pairs annotated. Leave the ticket open for resolution. Tool: linear_create_comment with issueId issue_pilot_monitoring or issue_audit_expenses, body containing the open items list.
OE 27 - Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com, with CC to samira.tariq@moveops.com, containing a tight summary of what still gates the expense categorizer relaunch. Content should cover: three Vectral false rejections now corrected (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130 changed to Approved), six duplicate stipend record pairs found and annotated across both accounts, Danielle Osei relocation contradiction still unresolved (Completed vs Cancelled), Kevin Tran duplicate relocation flagged, duplicate receipt investigation still open on ENG-220 (Kevin Tran and Andre Washington identical $89 keyboard receipts), pipeline hardening ENG-217 still in progress with 6 schema validation tests from ENG-212 pending integration into deployment gate, CRM route discrepancy for Andre Washington (deal says Columbus GA to Seattle WA, should be Detroit MI to Atlanta GA), and a 30-minute meeting booked early next week to walk the open list. Tool: send_email with sender samira.tariq@moveops.com, recipients [lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com], cc [samira.tariq@moveops.com].
OE 28 - Post the same relaunch gates summary to the engineering Slack channel (C003). At the end, include a short risks section listing client and reimbursement risks so account management and finance teams can follow up on their own: (1) Vectral Q3 contract renewal at risk with Rachel Whitfield citing ExpenseBot issues and demanding 15% discount (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at at_risk stage), (2) $885 in Mosaic false approvals absorbed by MoveOps per Elena directive (Andre Washington $400 desk overage plus $350 coworking plus Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking), (3) $89 duplicate receipt already paid to Kevin Tran at Vectral with matching submission from Andre Washington at Mosaic still on hold, (4) three Vectral employees had false rejections corrected today which may need client communication. Do not email account managers or finance directly per Samira's instruction. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003, payload containing the summary and risks section.
OE 29 - Create a 30-minute calendar event early next week on Samira's calendar with Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen as attendees. Early next week resolves to Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28, 2026 from the fixed universe date of April 26, 2026 (Sunday). Purpose is to walk through the open items list gating the expense categorizer relaunch: relocation record contradictions, duplicate stipend cleanup confirmation, duplicate receipt investigation status, and pipeline hardening completion criteria. Tool: calendar_add_calendar_event with title such as Expense Categorizer Relaunch Review, start_datetime on the chosen date, end_datetime 30 minutes later, attendees [lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com], description covering the agenda.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

verifier_env_task.task_idtask-snapshot-2t1vdo-multi-0b176694-verify

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

vm_created_at2026-04-03T16:46:10.359Z
🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search the Airtable Relocations table for all Vectral Systems records. Discovers Kevin Tran (recReloc00000003, Completed, Portland OR to Denver CO, move Apr 19-20) with a duplicate record (rec_reloc_tran, also Completed, Portland to Denver, RELOC-2026-005, created Jan 2026). Discovers Danielle Osei (recReloc00000004, Completed, Phoenix AZ to Denver CO, move Apr 26-27) with a contradicting record (rec_reloc_osei, Cancelled, Phoenix to Denver, RELOC-2026-010, notes say client pulled relocation due to budget reallocation, cancellation email sent to Heartland on 2/13). Also finds Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya (all In Progress). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value Vectral Systems.
OE 2 - Search the Airtable Relocations table for all Mosaic Robotics records. Discovers Andre Washington (recReloc00000009, Completed, Detroit MI to Atlanta GA, move Apr 26-27), Tanya Reeves (rec_reloc_reeves, Completed, San Diego to Seattle), Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta, Completed with damage claim on prototype), Lena Kowalski (recReloc00000000037, In Progress, Detroit to Salt Lake City), Andre Mitchell (recReloc00000000038, In Progress, Columbus OH to Boise ID), Deshawn Hartley (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver, In Progress, Tampa to Denver), and Keiko Tanaka (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle, In Progress, Columbus OH to Seattle). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value Mosaic Robotics.
OE 3 - Search the Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all Vectral Systems records. Discovers pilot-affected records including three that are still incorrectly rejected: Maria Santos $550 Standing Desk (recStip000000018, Rejected, review notes say "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot stale $400 default limit, Vectral policy allows $600, Pending manual correction"), James Okafor $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray (recStip000000019, Rejected, review notes say "Auto-rejected, double bug: miscategorized as office_supplies and used stale $50 limit, Pending manual correction"), Lisa Park $130 Mesh WiFi System (recStip000000020, Rejected, review notes say "Auto-rejected stale $100 default limit, Vectral policy allows $150, Pending manual correction"). Also discovers already-corrected records: Danielle Osei $475 Ergonomic Chair (recStip000000016, Approved, manually corrected by Mina), Kevin Tran $185 Internet Setup (recStip000000017, Approved, manually corrected by Mina). Discovers duplicate records for each: rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair ($475, Approved), rec_stipend_kevin_tran_internet ($185, Approved), rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk ($550, Pending Approval), rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi ($130, Pending Approval). Discovers rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard ($89 Ergonomic Keyboard, Approved, flagged Duplicate Hash with SHA-256 identical to Andre Washington submission rec_stipend_andre_keyboard). Plus clean post-pilot records for Kevin Tran (recStip000000004 chair $895, recStip000000005 coworking $250 properly rejected, recStip000000006 webcam $189, recStip000000014 monitor arm $129 Pending). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Account, value Vectral Systems.
OE 4 - Search the Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all Mosaic Robotics records. Discovers three false-approval records from the pilot: Andre Washington $1200 Standing Desk (recStip000000021, Approved in error, Mosaic policy caps ergonomic at $800, $400 over limit, config bug with string comparison), Andre Washington $350 Coworking Membership (recStip000000022, Approved in error, coworking explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy, config bug with excluded_categories stored as string not array), Kenji Nakamura $135 Coworking Day Passes (recStip000000023, Approved in error, same exclusion bug). All three note MoveOps absorbing costs per Elena directive. Discovers duplicate records: rec_stipend_andre_desk ($1200, Approved, duplicate of recStip000000021), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork ($135, Approved, duplicate of recStip000000023). Discovers rec_stipend_andre_keyboard ($89 Ergonomic Keyboard, Pending Approval, flagged Duplicate Hash identical to Kevin Tran submission). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Account, value Mosaic Robotics.
OE 5 - List Linear issues in the expense autocategorizer project to find all relevant engineering tickets. Discovers: issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211, Pilot Monitoring Vectral and Mosaic, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214, P1 Fix Vectral config, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215, P1 Fix Mosaic config, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216, Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses, state_in_progress, priority 0 urgent, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, Harden policy ingestion pipeline, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218, Dashboard type validation for policy config editor, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_anh_nguyen), issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219, Define launch readiness checklist for features with automated financial decisions, state_todo, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, Stipend duplicate detection receipt hash fingerprinting, state_todo, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212, Remaining edge case coverage, state_in_progress, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov). Tool: linear_list_issues with project proj_expense_autocategorizer.
OE 6 - Read comments on issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) to understand the full pilot incident timeline. Discovers: comment_false_rejection_incident (Samira reporting Danielle Osei $475 false rejection), comment_mina_two_incidents (Mina detailing Danielle Osei $475 chair auto-denied at $400 limit instead of $600, and Kevin Tran $185 internet auto-denied at $100 limit instead of $150, both manually overridden), comment_emeka_false_approvals (Emeka reporting Andre Washington $1200 desk approved despite $800 cap and $350 coworking approved despite exclusion, total risk at least $1,550), comment_monitoring_rca_update (Lena linking both incidents to policy ingestion pipeline ENG-209), comment_marcus_audit_request (Marcus demanding halt of auto-approvals and full audit), comment_elena_directive (Elena pausing auto-decisions, ordering full audit by 3/19, MoveOps absorbs $1,550, Mina to contact Vectral HR, Emeka to contact Mosaic HR), comment_samira_pause_confirmed (Samira confirming auto-decisions disabled March 18 for both clients). Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_pilot_monitoring.
OE 7 - Read comments on issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216) to find Dmitri's audit-related findings. Discovers comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery (2026-03-15) detailing three questionable receipt submissions found during the post-pause audit: Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, Paid 2026-03-08) and Andre Washington $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Pending) share identical SHA-256 hash a3f7c8d1e9b2, and Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, Pending, BrightLoop Analytics) shares same PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. Total flagged: $223 ($89 already paid out, $134 pending). Dmitri notes the existing system lacks duplicate receipt hash or PDF metadata detection. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_audit_expenses.
OE 8 - Read comments on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) to check the status of the duplicate receipt investigation. Discovers comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context (2026-03-15) noting: Airtable records identified as rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand. QuickBooks holds placed by Marcus on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme $45). AM verification requests sent to Vectral (Mina to Rachel Whitfield), Mosaic (Emeka to Tamara Byrd), and BrightLoop (Emeka to Tessa Moreno). Dmitri planned to begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation on Monday with Lena on ingestion pipeline changes. Ticket remains in state_todo. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_stipend_duplicate_detection.
OE 9 - Read comments on issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) and issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) to verify that config fixes were deployed. On ENG-214, discovers comment_lena_vectral_fix_plan (Lena beginning immediate config corrections, updating all Vectral limits from defaults to confirmed values, eliminating try/catch fallback). On ENG-215, discovers comment_lena_mosaic_fix_plan (correcting excluded_categories to array, ensuring max_amount fields are numbers, adding type guards) and comment_lena_deploy_verified (2026-03-18, posted on ENG-215) confirming both Vectral and Mosaic config corrections deployed to production with verification: Danielle Osei $475 ergonomic chair now correctly approved ($475 less than $600), Kevin Tran $185 internet correctly approved but setup fee edge case still needs separate ticket, Andre Washington $1200 desk correctly denied ($1200 greater than $800), Andre Washington coworking correctly denied (exclusion list working). Auto-decisions disabled so all route to review queue. Lena states she is closing ENG-214 and now starting ENG-217 pipeline hardening. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_p1_vectral_config, then linear_list_comments with issueId issue_p1_mosaic_malformed.
OE 10 - Read comments on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) to check current pipeline hardening status. Discovers comment_dmitri_audit_results (2026-03-20, posted on this issue) with the full audit: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct (83%), 5 false rejections (all Vectral: Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh wifi), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic: Andre Washington $1200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes), total financial risk $885. Also notes James Okafor revealed a categorization bug (keyboard tray not in ergonomic keyword list, separate from config problem). Ticket remains state_in_progress. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_pipeline_hardening.
OE 11 - Read comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) to discover the schema validation tests relevant to pipeline readiness. Discovers comment_dmitri_validation_tests (2026-03-20) listing 6 schema validation tests implemented in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback. All six pass with corrected configurations but all fail with original production configurations, confirming they would have caught both root causes before deployment. Lena is incorporating these tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate. Remaining ENG-212 work includes edge case tests for categorization, vendor abbreviation handling, and internet setup vs monthly distinction. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_edge_cases_backlog.
OE 12 - Search emails for threads related to the expense categorizer pilot, false rejections, and false approvals. Discovers: Samira's audit summary email to Elena listing 47 expenses with 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure; Marcus's email raising financial urgency about the Mosaic $1,550 incorrect approvals and demanding immediate halt; Emeka's email to Samira and Marcus reporting Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura false approvals with specific dollar amounts; and Samira's detailed audit spreadsheet email to Marcus confirming $885 total risk with MoveOps absorbing per Elena's directive. Tool: search_emails with query related to expense categorizer or pilot or false rejection or auto-approve.
OE 13 - Search emails for threads related to duplicate receipt findings and financial holds. Discovers: Dmitri's email to Marcus flagging three questionable receipt submissions (Kevin Tran $89, Andre Washington $89, Jordan Ekwueme $45 from TechComfort Supply), Marcus's reply confirming QuickBooks holds placed on STIP-2026-0094 ($89 Andre Washington, not yet disbursed) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45 Jordan Ekwueme, not yet disbursed) while Kevin Tran's $89 was already paid out on 2026-03-08, Marcus's email to Mina requesting Vectral HR verification of Kevin Tran's receipt legitimacy, Marcus's email to Emeka requesting Mosaic HR (Tamara Byrd) and BrightLoop HR (Tessa Moreno) verification, and Emeka's outreach email to Tamara Byrd about Andre Washington's $89 receipt. Tool: search_emails with query related to duplicate receipt or TechComfort or stipend hold or STIP-2026.
OE 14 - Search Slack messages in the engineering channel (C003) for discussions about the expense categorizer pilot. Discovers the full development and incident history: Samira's original proposal for auto-categorization, Chloe's operational requirements posted by Samira (Vectral: equipment $500, furniture $400, ergonomic $600, internet $150/mo, coworking excluded, office supplies $75; Mosaic: equipment $1000, furniture $800, ergonomic $800, internet $200/mo, coworking excluded, office supplies $100), Lena's architecture and keyword specifications, Dmitri's edge case test plans, progress updates, and the pilot launch and pause discussions. These confirm the correct policy limits that the pipeline should enforce. Tool: conversations_search_messages with search_query related to expense categorizer or pilot or ExpenseBot, or conversations_history with channel_id C003.
OE 15 - Search Slack or email for operations and billing threads about Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics that would affect how automated processing reads the records. Discovers the Heartland Movers Q1 billing discrepancy discussion in the operations channel (C006) where Chloe posted a full review and in the finance channel (C005) where Marcus logged the payment hold: Heartland billed for 8 moves at $1,600 each ($12,800) but only 5 are legitimate ($8,000), with 3 disputed ($4,800). Disputed moves include Danielle Osei (Vectral, Phoenix to Denver listed as Cancelled, RELOC-2026-010, confirming the cancellation also recorded in rec_reloc_osei), Lily Marchetti (Canopy Health, Nashville to Chicago, also Cancelled), and Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack, handled by Swift not Heartland). This Heartland billing thread cross-references and supports the Cancelled status for Danielle Osei's relocation. Tool: conversations_search_messages with search_query Heartland or billing Vectral Mosaic, or search_emails with query Heartland Q1 invoice.
OE 16 - Search CRM deals for Vectral Systems to check for client risk and billing context. Discovers deal_vectral_q2 ($11,000, Q2 2026 Relocations for Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei, stage contractsent), deal_vectral_april_2026 ($15,900, 3 April relocations for Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya, closedwon), and deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (At Risk stage, Rachel Whitfield demanded 15% discount citing three service failures: ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $89 keyboard flagged in fraud audit, and onboarding timeline issues under Catalina Dubois in Nov 2024). Rachel states Vectral is evaluating two competitor providers and needs a response by end of week for a board meeting where vendor renewal decisions will be presented. Mina is holding response pending internal alignment on counter-offer strategy. This represents active client and reimbursement risk for the risks section. Tool: crm_search_deals with company_id company_vectral or dealname Vectral.
OE 17 - Search CRM deals for Mosaic Robotics to check for data discrepancies and context. Discovers deal_mosaic_q2 ($4,500, stage closedwon, description says "Andre Washington, Robotics Engineer, from Columbus, GA to Seattle, WA") which contradicts the Airtable relocation record recReloc00000009 (Detroit MI to Atlanta GA) and Tamara Byrd's original email requesting relocation from Detroit to Atlanta. The CRM deal has the wrong origin city (Columbus GA instead of Detroit MI) and wrong destination city (Seattle WA instead of Atlanta GA). This discrepancy would cause automated processing to read the wrong route for this move. Tool: crm_search_deals with company_id company_mosaic or dealname Mosaic.
OE 18 - Look up contact details for Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen to retrieve their email addresses for the summary email. Discovers lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com (contact_id moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, Software Engineer, San Francisco), dmitri.volkov@moveops.com (contact_id moveops_dmitri_volkov, Software Engineer, San Francisco remote), anh.nguyen@moveops.com (contact_id moveops_anh_nguyen, Software Engineer, Austin remote). Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query Lena Bjorkstrom, then Dmitri Volkov, then Anh Nguyen.
OE 19 - Check Samira Tariq's calendar for early next week to find available time for a 30-minute meeting with Lena, Dmitri, and Anh. Early next week resolves to Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28, 2026 from the fixed universe date of April 26, 2026 (Sunday). Tool: calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime 2026-04-27 and end_datetime 2026-04-28 (or through end of April 28) to check for open slots.
OE 20 - Update the three still-rejected Vectral Systems stipend records to Approved status with corrective review notes, since proof is clear (all three have notes stating Pending manual correction and the correct Vectral policy limits confirm amounts are within policy). Records: recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550 Standing Desk, change Approval Status from Rejected to Approved, add note that Vectral ergonomic limit is $600 and $550 is within policy), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, change Approval Status from Rejected to Approved, add note that the pipeline miscategorized as office_supplies but the Airtable record already has the correct category Ergonomic Keyboard Tray and $82 is within Vectral ergonomic limit of $600), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130 Mesh WiFi System, change from Rejected to Approved, add note that Vectral internet equipment limit is $150 and $130 is within policy). Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001, records array containing the three updates.
OE 21 - Update the Danielle Osei relocation records in Airtable to flag the status contradiction. Record recReloc00000004 shows Status Completed with move dates Apr 26-27, but record rec_reloc_osei shows Status Cancelled with notes referencing RELOC-2026-010 and a cancellation email sent to Heartland on 2/13 due to budget reallocation. The Heartland Q1 billing dispute also lists this move as cancelled and disputed. Add review notes to both records flagging the contradiction so the pipeline does not treat the Completed record as authoritative without resolution. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01.
OE 22 - Update the duplicate Kevin Tran relocation records to flag the duplication. Record recReloc00000003 (created 2026-03-31, move dates Apr 19-20, Completed) and rec_reloc_tran (created 2026-01-08, move date Jan 26, RELOC-2026-005, Completed) both represent Kevin Tran at Vectral Systems relocating from Portland to Denver with the same coordinator Javier Morales. Add review notes to both records identifying them as duplicate entries for the same relocation so the pipeline does not count or process this move twice. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01.
OE 23 - Update the duplicate stipend record pairs to flag them as duplicates that the pipeline should not process as separate transactions. Six pairs identified: (1) Kevin Tran Internet $185, recStip000000017 and rec_stipend_kevin_tran_internet, (2) Danielle Osei Chair $475, recStip000000016 and rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair, (3) Maria Santos Desk $550, recStip000000018 and rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, (4) Lisa Park WiFi $130, recStip000000020 and rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi, (5) Andre Washington Desk $1200, recStip000000021 and rec_stipend_andre_desk, (6) Kenji Nakamura Coworking $135, recStip000000023 and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork. Add review notes to each record flagging it as a duplicate pair entry. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001 (multiple calls needed, max 10 records per call).
OE 24 - Update the duplicate receipt and audit-hold stipend records to ensure they do not read as clean for automated processing. Records: rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $89, already flagged Duplicate Hash, already Paid on 2026-03-08), rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Andre Washington, Mosaic, $89, flagged Duplicate Hash, Pending Approval, QuickBooks hold STIP-2026-0094). Add or reinforce review notes indicating these are under active duplicate receipt investigation (ENG-220) and should not be auto-processed. The three Mosaic false-approval records (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023) already have detailed review notes documenting the errors and Elena's directive to absorb costs, so they are already marked as audit records. Tool: airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001.
OE 25 - Create a comment on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) documenting the 6 duplicate stipend record pairs discovered during the release readiness pass, the status of the cross-company duplicate receipt investigation (Kevin Tran Vectral $89 and Andre Washington Mosaic $89 with identical SHA-256 hash, Kevin Tran already paid out), and the current Airtable annotation status. Note that this ticket should remain open until the hash fingerprinting system is implemented and all duplicate pairs are fully reconciled. Tool: linear_create_comment with issueId issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, body containing the findings summary.
OE 26 - Create a comment on issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) or issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216) documenting open items found during the release readiness pass that block relaunch: (1) Danielle Osei relocation status contradiction between recReloc00000004 (Completed) and rec_reloc_osei (Cancelled), supported by Heartland Q1 billing dispute, (2) Kevin Tran duplicate relocation records recReloc00000003 and rec_reloc_tran, (3) Andre Washington CRM deal discrepancy where deal_mosaic_q2 lists route as Columbus GA to Seattle WA but actual route per Airtable and email is Detroit MI to Atlanta GA, (4) three false rejections now corrected (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park), (5) six duplicate stipend pairs annotated. Leave the ticket open for resolution. Tool: linear_create_comment with issueId issue_pilot_monitoring or issue_audit_expenses, body containing the open items list.
OE 27 - Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com, with CC to samira.tariq@moveops.com, containing a tight summary of what still gates the expense categorizer relaunch. Content should cover: three Vectral false rejections now corrected (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130 changed to Approved), six duplicate stipend record pairs found and annotated across both accounts, Danielle Osei relocation contradiction still unresolved (Completed vs Cancelled), Kevin Tran duplicate relocation flagged, duplicate receipt investigation still open on ENG-220 (Kevin Tran and Andre Washington identical $89 keyboard receipts), pipeline hardening ENG-217 still in progress with 6 schema validation tests from ENG-212 pending integration into deployment gate, CRM route discrepancy for Andre Washington (deal says Columbus GA to Seattle WA, should be Detroit MI to Atlanta GA), and a 30-minute meeting booked early next week to walk the open list. Tool: send_email with sender samira.tariq@moveops.com, recipients [lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com], cc [samira.tariq@moveops.com].
OE 28 - Post the same relaunch gates summary to the engineering Slack channel (C003). At the end, include a short risks section listing client and reimbursement risks so account management and finance teams can follow up on their own: (1) Vectral Q3 contract renewal at risk with Rachel Whitfield citing ExpenseBot issues and demanding 15% discount (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at at_risk stage), (2) $885 in Mosaic false approvals absorbed by MoveOps per Elena directive (Andre Washington $400 desk overage plus $350 coworking plus Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking), (3) $89 duplicate receipt already paid to Kevin Tran at Vectral with matching submission from Andre Washington at Mosaic still on hold, (4) three Vectral employees had false rejections corrected today which may need client communication. Do not email account managers or finance directly per Samira's instruction. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003, payload containing the summary and risks section.
OE 29 - Create a 30-minute calendar event early next week on Samira's calendar with Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen as attendees. Early next week resolves to Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28, 2026 from the fixed universe date of April 26, 2026 (Sunday). Purpose is to walk through the open items list gating the expense categorizer relaunch: relocation record contradictions, duplicate stipend cleanup confirmation, duplicate receipt investigation status, and pipeline hardening completion criteria. Tool: calendar_add_calendar_event with title such as Expense Categorizer Relaunch Review, start_datetime on the chosen date, end_datetime 30 minutes later, attendees [lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com], description covering the agenda.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

9 The Danielle Osei relocation record recReloc00000004 (showing Completed status for Phoenix AZ to Denver CO, move Apr 26-27) was updated or annotated (via airtable_update_records) to flag the status contradiction with rec_reloc_osei (showing Cancelled).
outcome
 ✗✗✓✓✗✓ 50%
✗Run #1No airtable_update_records call was made for recReloc00000004. The Danielle Osei relocation contradiction was mentioned in a Linear comment on issue_pilot_monitoring but the actual Airtable record was never updated or annotated.
✗Run #2The airtable_update_records call on the Relocations table only updated recReloc00000003 and rec_reloc_osei. recReloc00000004 (the Completed record for Danielle Osei's April move) was never annotated to flag the contradiction with the Cancelled record.
✓Run #3airtable_update_records updated recReloc00000004 Status to 'Cancelled' and added notes explicitly referencing the contradiction: 'Was incorrectly marked Completed. Multiple sources confirm this relocation was CANCELLED... See rec_reloc_osei (RELOC-2026-010), Heartland Q1 invoice dispute, and Chloe Vance's operations audit.'
✓Run #4airtable_update_records call targeting recReloc00000004 changes Status to 'In Progress' and adds correction notes explicitly referencing the contradiction: 'Prior relocation for same employee (rec_reloc_osei) was Cancelled in Feb 2026 due to budget reallocation.'
✗Run #5The agent updated recReloc00000004 by changing the status to 'In Progress' (interpreting the issue as a timing problem — move hadn't started yet), but did NOT flag the status contradiction with rec_reloc_osei (Cancelled). The criterion requires flagging the Completed vs. Cancelled contradiction between the two records, which was not done.
✓Run #6airtable_update_records was called on tblRelocations01 targeting recReloc00000004 with a detailed release readiness flag noting the status contradiction with rec_reloc_osei (Cancelled) and the February cancellation confirmation.
10 The Danielle Osei relocation record rec_reloc_osei (showing Cancelled status for Phoenix to Denver, RELOC-2026-010) was updated or annotated (via airtable_update_records) to flag the status contradiction with recReloc00000004 (showing Completed)
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1No airtable_update_records call was made for rec_reloc_osei. The contradiction was noted in a Linear comment but not patched in the Airtable relocation record itself.
✓Run #2airtable_update_records on tblRelocations01 updated rec_reloc_osei with notes referencing the billing discrepancy, the Cancelled status, and the separate active relocation recReloc00000004, flagging the contradiction.
✗Run #3The trajectory shows rec_reloc_osei was never updated or annotated. Only recReloc00000004 and recReloc00000003 were updated in the relocation update call. The criterion requires both records to be cross-referenced, but rec_reloc_osei received no update.
✗Run #4No airtable_update_records call targets rec_reloc_osei. Only recReloc00000004 was updated for Danielle Osei's relocation; the Cancelled counterpart record was not annotated.
✗Run #5The agent never updated or annotated rec_reloc_osei. No airtable_update_records call references this record ID. The Danielle Osei cancelled record was not cross-referenced or flagged.
✗Run #6The agent only updated recReloc00000004 and recReloc00000003 in the Relocations table. rec_reloc_osei (the Cancelled record) was never targeted by airtable_update_records, so the Cancelled counterpart was not annotated to flag the contradiction.

✓Run #6conversations_search_messages was called with query 'cancelled vendor changed Danielle Osei', which directly targets the Danielle Osei cancellation context needed to find the Heartland billing dispute and related operations threads.
